usage: Create forensic disk images and preview evidence without altering it.
description: Disk imaging and evidence preview tool. Creates forensically sound images in raw, E01 and AFF formats, with on-the-fly hashing for chain of custody on NTFS and FAT volumes.

usage: Patched dd for forensic disk acquisition with built-in hashing.
description: Forensic fork of dd that adds on-the-fly hashing, progress reporting, and error logging while imaging a disk or partition. Common first step in disk forensics.

usage: Recover data from failing disks into a forensic image.
description: Data recovery tool that copies a disk or partition, skipping and retrying bad sectors, useful for imaging damaged media before file system analysis.

usage: Recover lost partitions and repair partition tables.
description: Recovers lost partitions and rebuilds boot sectors on NTFS, FAT and ext file systems. Pairs with PhotoRec for file carving from a disk image.

usage: Carve deleted files from a disk image by signature.
description: File carving and recovery tool that ignores the file system and recovers files by their signatures, useful for recovering deleted evidence from a partition image.

usage: Signature-based file carving from raw images.
description: Carves files such as JPG, PDF and DOC out of a raw disk image based on headers and footers. A staple for recovering deleted files during disk forensics.

usage: Fast file carver based on header/footer definitions.
description: High-performance file carving tool derived from Foremost, recovering files from a disk image or unallocated space using a configurable signature database.

usage: Analyse and extract embedded files and firmware.
description: Firmware analysis tool that scans a binary image for embedded files and executable code, extracting partitions and file systems for further examination.

usage: Scan an image for emails, URLs, credit cards and other features.
description: Scans a disk image and extracts features such as email addresses, URLs, IPs and credit-card numbers without parsing the file system, producing histograms for triage.

usage: Extract processes, network connections and artefacts from a RAM dump.
description: Advanced memory forensics framework that parses a volatile memory dump to list processes, open handles, network connections and injected code on Windows, Linux and macOS.

usage: Memory analysis framework for incident response.
description: Memory analysis framework that examines RAM dumps to reconstruct process lists, kernel structures and malware artefacts during incident response.

usage: Acquire physical memory from a live Windows host.
description: Open-source memory acquisition driver that captures physical RAM from a running Windows machine into a raw or AFF4 dump for later volatile analysis.

usage: Capture volatile memory from a Linux or Android device.
description: Loadable kernel module that dumps full physical memory from a Linux or Android system, preserving volatile process and kernel state for forensic analysis.

usage: Acquire memory from Linux cloud VMs.
description: Portable Linux memory acquisition tool from Microsoft that captures RAM from cloud and on-prem hosts without compiling against the running kernel.

usage: Mount a memory dump as a browsable file system.
description: Presents the contents of a physical memory dump or live RAM as a virtual file system, exposing processes, handles and kernel heap for easy volatile analysis.

usage: Capture and inspect network packets interactively.
description: The standard network protocol analyser. Captures live traffic or opens a PCAP, dissecting hundreds of protocols for deep packet inspection during network forensics.

usage: Command-line packet capture and PCAP analysis.
description: Terminal version of Wireshark for capturing live traffic and filtering PCAP files in scripts, extracting fields and following TCP streams during network forensics.

usage: Lightweight packet capture on the command line.
description: Command-line packet sniffer that captures network traffic to a PCAP file using BPF filters, the go-to first tool for collecting evidence of network activity.

usage: Extract files and credentials from captured traffic.
description: Network forensic analysis tool that parses a PCAP to reassemble transferred files, images and credentials, and to fingerprint hosts seen in the traffic.

usage: Generate rich network logs from traffic.
description: Network security monitor that turns raw traffic into structured logs of connections, DNS, HTTP and TLS activity, widely used for network forensics and threat hunting.

usage: IDS/IPS and PCAP analysis with rule matching.
description: High-performance network IDS/IPS engine that inspects live traffic or a PCAP against signatures, flagging indicators of compromise in network forensics.

usage: Index and search large volumes of captured traffic.
description: Large-scale full packet capture and indexing system that stores PCAP and lets analysts search and pivot across billions of network sessions.

usage: Reverse-engineer and decompile malware binaries.
description: Open-source software reverse engineering suite from the NSA. Disassembles and decompiles executables across many architectures for static malware analysis.

usage: Command-line reverse engineering framework.
description: Scriptable reverse-engineering framework for disassembling, debugging and patching binaries, used for static and dynamic malware analysis from the terminal.

usage: GUI front-end for reverse engineering with Rizin.
description: Graphical reverse-engineering platform built on Rizin, offering disassembly, decompilation and graph views for analysing malware samples.

usage: Debug Windows malware dynamically.
description: Open-source x64/x32 debugger for Windows used to step through malware at runtime, inspect memory and unpack samples during dynamic analysis.

usage: Match files against malware signature rules.
description: Pattern-matching engine that classifies and identifies malware samples using rules describing textual or binary signatures, central to malware triage.

usage: Automated dynamic malware analysis in a sandbox.
description: Automated malware analysis sandbox that runs a suspicious file in an isolated VM and reports behaviour, dropped files, network traffic and IOCs.

usage: Statically triage Windows executables for malware indicators.
description: Static analysis tool that inspects a Windows PE file for suspicious imports, strings, entropy and known indicators without executing it.

usage: Extract data from mobile phones and tablets.
description: Commercial mobile forensics platform that performs logical and physical extraction from smartphones, recovering messages, call logs and app data from Android and iOS.

usage: Mobile device acquisition and decoding.
description: Mobile forensics suite for extracting and decoding data from phones, SIM cards and tablets, supporting a wide range of Android and iOS devices.

usage: Acquire and decode Android device data.
description: Collection of forensic tools for Android that performs logical extraction, decodes app databases and cracks lockscreen patterns from a device or backup.

usage: Parse iOS logs, events and app artefacts.
description: iOS Logs, Events And Plists Parser that processes a file-system extraction from an iPhone and produces an HTML report of artefacts for mobile forensics.

usage: Parse Android logs, events and protobuf artefacts.
description: Android Logs Events And Protobuf Parser that processes an Android extraction and reports app usage, locations and other artefacts for mobile forensics.

usage: Communicate with iOS devices over USB.
description: Cross-platform library and utilities to talk to iPhones and iPads without iTunes, used to create backups and pull device information for iOS forensics.

usage: Build a super timeline from disk and log artefacts.
description: Engine that automatically extracts timestamps from a disk image, logs and artefacts to build a combined super timeline for timeline analysis.

usage: Collaboratively analyse forensic timelines.
description: Web-based collaborative timeline analysis tool that ingests Plaso output and lets investigators search, tag and annotate events across a case.

usage: Fast Windows event-log timeline and threat hunting.
description: Sigma-based tool that scans Windows event logs to generate a forensic timeline and flag suspicious activity, aiding timeline analysis and threat hunting.

usage: Hunt through Windows event logs with Sigma rules.
description: Rapidly searches and hunts through Windows event logs and the MFT using Sigma rules, surfacing suspicious events for timeline and incident analysis.

usage: Extract artefacts from Windows registry hives.
description: Plugin-driven tool that parses Windows registry hives to pull out user activity, USB history, run keys and other artefacts for disk forensics.

usage: Rapidly collect and process triage artefacts.
description: Kroll Artifact Parser and Extractor that quickly collects key forensic artefacts from a live Windows system or image and runs parsers against them.

usage: Endpoint visibility and remote forensic collection.
description: Endpoint monitoring and digital forensics platform that hunts across many machines at once, collecting artefacts and memory using a flexible query language.

usage: Read and write file metadata across formats.
description: Reads, writes and edits metadata in images, documents and media files, exposing EXIF, GPS and timestamp metadata that is central to file-level analysis.

usage: Compute and audit hash sets for evidence.
description: Computes MD5, SHA-1 and SHA-256 hashes recursively and audits a set of files against a known hash list, supporting chain-of-custody verification.

usage: Assess and collect evidence from cloud accounts.
description: Open-source tool that audits AWS, Azure and GCP accounts against security best practices and pulls configuration evidence for cloud forensics.

usage: Multi-cloud security posture and evidence collection.
description: Multi-cloud auditing tool that queries AWS, Azure and GCP APIs to build a snapshot of configuration and access, useful for cloud incident investigations.

usage: Trace and visualise cryptocurrency transactions.
description: Open-source cryptoasset analytics platform that clusters addresses and traces Bitcoin and Ethereum transaction flows for blockchain forensics.

usage: Visual link analysis across OSINT data sources.
description: Link-analysis and OSINT tool that maps relationships between people, domains, IPs and infrastructure using transforms, used for open-source intelligence investigations.

usage: Automate OSINT collection on a target.
description: OSINT automation tool that queries over a hundred public data sources to gather intelligence on domains, IPs, emails and people for open-source investigations.

usage: Gather emails, hosts and subdomains for a domain.
description: OSINT reconnaissance tool that collects email addresses, subdomains, hosts and open ports for a target domain from public sources and search engines.

usage: Modular web reconnaissance framework.
description: Full-featured OSINT and web reconnaissance framework with a modular workflow for harvesting open-source intelligence about a target.

usage: Extract and analyse phone and cloud data.
description: Mobile forensics suite that extracts data from phones, SIM cards and cloud accounts, decoding messages, contacts and app data from Android and iOS devices.
